title: The Paradise Mystery
description: The Paradise Mystery — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Paradise Mystery" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: "Alexander Chilstone"
memes.description: "Manager of the London & Colonies Bank in Threadneedle Street who provided key testimony at the inquest about John Braden's financial affairs, including the deposit of ten thousand pounds just before Braden's death. (006-034)(006-036)"

memes.label: "Ambrose Campany"
memes.description: "The librarian of the Dean and Chapter of Wrychester, custodian of one of the most famous cathedral libraries in England, and the man who first identifies the Barthorpe history book as a vital clue to John Braden's origins. (005-036)(005-052)"

relations.label: Library of the Dean and Chapter of Wrychester

memes.label: "Archdale"
memes.description: "A young architect, member of a well-known Wrychester firm of architects and a member of the Wrychester Club, whose expert testimony about the cathedral gallery flooring swayed the coroner's jury to a verdict of death by misadventure. (005-024)(006-041)"

memes.label: Arrest and Trial of John Brake
memes.description: The arrest and trial of John Brake (alias of John Braden) for embezzlement at Spelbank, the backstory crime that set the entire Paradise mystery in motion. (009-027)(014-039)

relations.label: 围绕其死因展开的调查

memes.label: "Barthorpe"
memes.description: "An ancient market-town in northern Leicestershire, two miles from Braden Medworth — the place that holds the key to John Braden's true identity through an antiquarian book titled 'History of Barthorpe,' and the site of Bryce's pivotal investigative journey that uncovers the connection between Ransford, Brake, and Bewery. (004-063)(004-061)"

memes.label: Betty Campany
memes.description: Daughter of Ambrose Campany, the librarian to the Dean and Chapter of Wrychester Cathedral, and love interest of Dick Bewery. (005-036)

memes.label: "Braden Medworth"
memes.description: "A small, quiet, and picturesque village two miles from Barthorpe in Leicestershire — the place where Bryce discovers the marriage register entry linking John Brake, Mary Bewery, and Mark Ransford, providing the crucial genealogical key to the Paradise mystery. (008-016)(008-024)"

memes.label: "Cathedral Close"
memes.description: "The enclosed precinct surrounding Wrychester Cathedral — a tranquil, residential quarter of ancient buildings and green spaces that becomes the setting for death, investigation, and the hidden tensions of a community bound by proximity and secrets. (001-001)(002-035)"

relations.label: John Braden fell to his death

memes.label: "Cathedral Library"
memes.description: "The famous Library of the Dean and Chapter of Wrychester — an ancient building in one corner of the Close housing priceless volumes, manuscripts, and medieval relics, where the librarian Ambrose Campany sets in motion a key clue in the Paradise mystery. (005-051)"

memes.label: "Chapter 1: Only the Guardian"

memes.label: "Chapter 3: St. Wrytha’s Stair"
memes.description: "Bryce is summarily dismissed by Dr. Ransford after discovering the cheque, and the mysterious death at St. Wrytha's Stair begins to draw the dismissed assistant into his own investigation of the Paradise mystery."

memes.label: "Chapter 4: The Room at the Mitre"
memes.description: The Room at the Mitre

memes.description: The Scrap of Paper

memes.label: "Chapter 10: Diplomacy"
memes.description: "Bryce returns to Wrychester convinced of Ransford's guilt, and begins a calculated campaign of manipulation — waylaying Mary Bewery on the golf links and deploying \"diplomacy\" to leverage his knowledge of her guardian's secret toward winning her hand."

memes.label: "Chapter 11: The Back Room"
memes.description: The Back Room

memes.label: "Chapter 12: Murder of the Mason’s Labourer"
memes.description: Murder of the Mason’s Labourer

memes.description: The Double Offer

memes.label: "Chapter 16: Beforehand"
memes.description: Beforehand

memes.label: "Chapter 17: To Be Shadowed"
memes.description: To Be Shadowed

memes.label: "Chapter 18: Surprise"
memes.description: "Mitchington and the Scotland Yard detective question Ransford, whose guarded revelations put a surprising new complexion on the case, while Bryce pursues his own parallel investigation into the mystery."

memes.label: "Chapter 19: The Subtlety of the Devil"
memes.description: The Subtlety of the Devil

memes.label: "Chapter 20: Jettison Takes a Hand"
memes.description: Jettison Takes a Hand

memes.label: "Chapter 21: The Saxonsteade Arms"
memes.description: The Saxonsteade Arms

memes.label: "Chapter 22: Other People’s Notions"
memes.description: Other People’s Notions

memes.label: "Chapter 23: The Unexpected"
memes.description: The Unexpected

memes.label: "Chapter 24: Finesse"
memes.description: "Glassdale visits a Wrychester solicitor regarding the reward for the Saxonsteade jewels, while Bryce deploys \"finesse\" in his scheme to pressure Ransford through Mary Bewery — a strategic game that draws Glassdale's knowledge into the centre of the affair."

memes.label: "Chapter 25: The Old Well House"
memes.description: The Old Well House

memes.label: "Chapter 26: The Other Man"
memes.description: The Other Man

memes.label: "Chapter 27: The Guarded Secret"
memes.description: The Guarded Secret

memes.label: "Charles Claybourne"
memes.description: "The sexton and shoemaker of Braden Medworth, a key witness to the marriage of John Brake and Mary Bewery, and the source of crucial backstory information for Pemberton Bryce's investigation. (008-024)(008-026)"

memes.label: "Collishaw"
memes.description: "A Wrychester labourer poisoned to death in the Cathedral, the second suspicious death after John Braden — killed because he knew too much. (011-058)(012-016)"

memes.label: "Coroner's Inquest"
memes.description: "The formal legal body convened to determine the cause of death in the Paradise and Collishaw cases — a public forum where evidence is presented and provisional verdicts are returned, but where the deeper truths of the novel remain concealed behind the limits of legal testimony. (004-015)(006-022)"

memes.label: "Criminal Investigation"
memes.description: "The multi-layered process of inquiry into the suspicious deaths in Wrychester — encompassing official police procedure, the coroner's inquest, and parallel amateur investigations — which drives the plot and reveals the novel's concern with how truth is discovered and concealed. (002-046)(004-027)"

memes.label: Dean and Chapter of Wrychester
memes.description: The ecclesiastical governing body of Wrychester Cathedral, responsible for the cathedral's administration, its famous library, and the management of the Paradise precinct. (001-008)(005-051)

relations.label: Braden 死亡案的验尸官调查

memes.label: "Death of Collishaw"
memes.description: "The second suspicious death in Wrychester — the poisoning of a mason's labourer who possessed dangerous knowledge about the Paradise affair — which confirms that the earlier death was no accident and that a murderer is actively protecting a hidden past. (012-027)(012-030)"

memes.label: "Death of John Braden"
memes.description: "The inciting event of the novel — the discovery of a dying stranger at the foot of St. Wrytha's Stair in Paradise — whose ambiguous circumstances launch the intertwined investigations that drive the plot and conceal a deeper secret about the dead man's true identity. (003-034)"
aliases: the fall from St. Wrytha's Stair

memes.label: "Christopher Dellingham"
memes.description: "A London antiquarian and tourist who accompanied John Braden to Wrychester, becoming a key witness at the inquest into Braden's death, and whose true identity is later questioned. (004-037)(004-039)"

memes.label: "Dick Bewery"
memes.description: "Dr. Ransford's seventeen-year-old ward — a schoolboy whose discovery of John Braden's body sets the mystery in motion and whose coming of age threatens the secrecy of his guardian's household. (001-008)(005-036)"

memes.label: Discovery of the Scrap of Paper
memes.description: The discovery of a curiously folded scrap of paper in John Braden's purse, a piece of evidence that becomes the central object driving Pemberton Bryce's investigation into Braden's death. (003-054)(004-067)

relations.label: Arrest and Trial of John Brake

memes.label: Dr. Coates
memes.description: Elderly leading medical practitioner of Wrychester who, together with Dr. Everest, performed the postmortem on Collishaw and confirmed poisoning by hydrocyanic acid as the cause of death (012-034).

memes.label: Dr. Everest
memes.description: A Wrychester medical doctor who assisted Dr. Coates in performing the postmortem examination on James Collishaw, confirming death by hydrogen cyanide poisoning. (012-054)

memes.label: "Duke of Saxonsteade"
memes.description: "The great local magnate of Wrychester, owner of the Saxonsteade estate; his family was the victim of a famous unsolved jewel robbery, and his London estate agent Glassdale was a forger who later held the key to recovering the stolen gems. (006-025)(005-007)"

memes.label: Events
memes.description: Directory of all event entries in The Paradise Mystery wiki, dynamically generated.

memes.label: Exposure of Falkiner Wraye
memes.description: The dramatic revelation that Stephen Folliot, a respected Wrychester resident, is in truth Falkiner Wraye — the man John Braden had been hunting for years, and the co-conspirator in the Spelbank fraud. (014-043)(021-040)

relations.label: Arrest and Trial of John Brake

memes.label: "Falkiner Wraye"
memes.description: "A Barthorpe estate agent who, with his partner Flood, defrauded John Brake of bank funds; later assumed the identity of Stephen Folliot and settled in Wrychester. (014-043)(021-040)"

relations.label: Arrest and Trial of John Brake

memes.label: "Fladgate"
memes.description: "A verger at Wrychester Cathedral whose appointment was secured by Falkiner Wraye (alias Folliot); his true name is Flood, and he was a co-conspirator in the fraud that ruined John Brake, later turning informant to save himself (027-013)(027-037)."

memes.label: "Friary Lane"
memes.description: "A narrow lane at the back of Wrychester Cathedral Close, lined with old houses — home to Pemberton Bryce and a setting where footsteps, ancient masonry, and the proximity of neighbours matter to the unfolding mystery. (004-067)(005-051)"

memes.label: George Stebbing
memes.description: Young, sandy-haired, freckled secretary of the Second Friendly Society in Wrychester who provided Jettison with key information about Glassdale's suspicious deposit of fifty pounds in gold (020-012).

memes.label: "Reverend Thomas Gilwaters"
memes.description: "A scholarly clergyman and former vicar of Wrychester, who held the living before Dr. Ransford and possesses critical knowledge about the Braden family's secret past. (008-049)(008-051)"

memes.label: "Glassdale"
memes.description: "A former convict and fellow-inmate of Brake who holds crucial knowledge about the Duke of Saxonsteade's jewels and becomes a key figure in the resolution of the Paradise mystery. (014-056)(015-007)"

memes.label: "Guardianship"
memes.description: "The legal and moral relationship through which Dr. Mark Ransford serves as protector and surrogate parent to Dick and Mary Bewery, concealing the truth of their parentage. (001-047)(009-044)"

memes.label: "Detective-Sergeant Jettison"
memes.description: "A detective-sergeant from Scotland Yard sent to Wrychester to assist the local police in solving the two mysterious deaths, whose professional experience and methodical approach provide a crucial outside perspective on the case. (017-028)(017-039)"

memes.label: "John Braden"
memes.description: "The man found dead at the foot of St. Wrytha's Stair whose dual identity as John Braden (in Wrychester) and John Brake (a convicted bank fraudster) is the central mystery of the novel. (004-037)(005-006)"

memes.label: John Brake
memes.description: Former bank manager who married Mary Bewery in 1891, was convicted of embezzlement and served ten years' penal servitude, then returned to Wrychester under the alias "John Braden" seeking the men who ruined him — only to die at the foot of St. Wrytha's Stair (008-024).

memes.label: "Mark Ransford"
memes.description: "A physician and guardian of Dick and Mary Bewery, who harbours a dangerous secret about the dead man's identity — a secret that places him at the centre of the Paradise mystery and tests the limits of protective deception. (001-003)"

memes.label: "Mary Bewery"
memes.description: "Ransford's ward and the object of romantic attention from both Pemberton Bryce and her guardian, whose hidden parentage lies at the heart of the Paradise mystery. (002-024)(005-012)(010-001)"

memes.label: "Detective-Sergeant Mitchington"
memes.description: "The sharp-eyed Wrychester police detective who leads the official investigation into John Braden's death at Paradise. (003-054)(003-056)"

memes.label: "Monday Market"
memes.description: "A small cobbled square in Wrychester, dominated on one side by the Mitre Hotel, where key early scenes of the Paradise mystery investigation unfold. (004-030)"

memes.label: "Mr. Folliot"
memes.description: "A wealthy resident of the Close and stepfather of Sackville Bonham, whose testimony at the inquest and social connections place him at the intersection of Wrychester society and the Paradise mystery. (002-070)"

memes.label: "Mr. Harker"
memes.description: "A retired police detective whose professional investigation of the Paradise death, conducted outside official channels, ultimately uncovers the truth that the inquest could not. (006-001)(014-033)"

memes.label: Mrs. Batts
memes.description: Next-door neighbour of the Collishaw family whose testimony to Inspector Mitchington placed Dr. Ransford alone with Collishaw's dinner shortly before the poisoning (012-041).

memes.label: "Mrs. Deramore"
memes.description: "A neighbour of Dr. Ransford in the Wrychester Close whose eyewitness observation of John Braden crossing the Close triggered the rumour that Braden had visited Ransford before his death. (007-019)(007-020)"

memes.label: "Mrs. Folliot"
memes.description: "The domineering matriarch of Wrychester society, wife of Mr. Folliot, mother of Sackville Bonham, and the principal engine of gossip and suspicion against Dr. Ransford. (002-071)(007-010)"

memes.label: "Mrs. Partingley"
memes.description: "The landlady of the Mitre Inn in Wrychester, whose testimony about John Braden's arrival and his travelling companion Christopher Dellingham provides the foundational facts of the inquest. (004-030)(004-036)"

memes.label: "The Old Well House"
memes.description: "An ancient stone well-house in the garden of Stephen Folliot's residence in the Cathedral Close, converted into a private retreat and the scene of the novel's final confrontation and deaths. (025-006)(025-073)"

memes.label: "Paradise"
memes.description: "The specific location within Wrychester Cathedral Close where John Braden's body is discovered at the foot of St. Wrytha's Stair — a name whose irony defines the novel's thematic structure. (003-029)(004-004)"

memes.label: "Pemberton Bryce"
memes.description: "Ransford's ambitious young assistant, whose pursuit of Mary Bewery and investigation of the Paradise death make him the primary antagonist of the novel. (001-030)(002-001)"

memes.label: People
memes.description: Directory of all person entries in The Paradise Mystery wiki, dynamically generated.

memes.label: "Dr. Ransford's House"
memes.description: "The Wrychester residence and medical practice of Dr. Mark Ransford, located in the Cathedral Close, which serves as both a domestic setting for the Ransford–Bewery ménage and a key location in the mystery surrounding the death of John Braden (001-002)(001-003)."

memes.label: Richard Jenkins
memes.description: A 17th-century Welshman buried in Paradise whose tomb, marked by a coat of arms bearing three crows, became the hiding place of the Saxonsteade jewels and the key to the scrap-of-paper mystery. (005-005)

memes.label: "Sackville Bonham"
memes.description: "Stepson of Mr. Folliot, Mary Bewery's persistent suitor, and an unwitting pawn in Pemberton Bryce's schemes — a loquacious young man of Wrychester society (002-070)(003-021)."

memes.label: "Saxonsteade"
memes.description: "The country estate of the Duke of Saxonsteade, the great local magnate whose connections to the Paradise mystery and to John Braden's true identity make his residence a key location in the investigation. (004-039)(005-007)"

memes.label: Saxonsteade Arms
memes.description: A village inn in Saxonsteade where characters meet and stay during the investigation of the Paradise mystery and the Saxonsteade jewels. (022-001)

memes.label: "The Scrap of Paper"
memes.description: "A crucial piece of evidence found in John Braden's purse after his death, containing a secret that drives the central conflict of the mystery (003-054)(005-002)."

memes.label: "Secrecy and Concealment"
memes.description: "The deliberate withholding of information — about identity, crime, and motive — that drives the plot of The Paradise Mystery and defines the moral landscape of its characters, from protective concealment born of love to strategic secrecy deployed as a weapon (002-002)(017-061)."

memes.label: "St. Wrytha's Stair"
memes.description: "The ancient stone stairway in the south side of Wrychester Cathedral from whose head John Braden fell forty feet to his death in the Paradise garden below. (003-001)(003-034)"

memes.label: "Suspicion and Distrust"
memes.description: "The atmosphere of mutual suspicion that pervades Wrychester following the death at Paradise, in which every character becomes both suspect and investigator (006-022)(007-001)."

memes.label: "The Close"
memes.description: "The cathedral close of Wrychester, a gated precinct housing the Deanery and wealthy residents, where several key scenes of the investigation unfold. (001-001)"

memes.label: "The Deanery"
memes.description: "The official residence of the Dean within Wrychester Cathedral Close — a place of clerical authority where Dr. Ransford attends professional calls and whose proximity to Paradise makes it part of the novel's geography of observation and concealment (003-011)(003-015)."

memes.label: "The Mitre Hotel"
memes.description: "The hotel in Wrychester where John Braden stayed under the name John Braden; managed by Mrs. Partingley, it became the starting point of the police investigation into his death (004-028)(005-006)."

memes.label: The Paradise Mystery
memes.description: A 1920 mystery novel by J. S. Fletcher set in the cathedral town of Wrychester, where a young doctor attempts to turn a mysterious death to his romantic advantage — a tale of secrets, guardianship, and murder in the shadow of a thirteenth-century cathedral.

memes.label: "Varner"
memes.description: "A mason working at Wrychester Cathedral who becomes the most important eyewitness to the death of John Braden, having seen the fatal fall from St. Wrytha's Stair. (003-031)(005-017)"

memes.label: "Wrychester"
memes.description: "The fictional English cathedral town that serves as the primary setting of The Paradise Mystery — a place of old-world tranquillity whose serene surface conceals crime, deception, and sudden death (001-001)(001-002)."

memes.label: "Wrychester Cathedral"
memes.description: "The thirteenth-century Cathedral at the heart of Wrychester, whose towering spire dominates the skyline and whose ancient precinct — the Close — provides the setting for the Paradise mystery's central events (001-001)(001-002)."

memes.label: "Wrychester Club"
memes.description: "An exclusive private club in the centre of Wrychester, drawing its membership from the city's professional and leisured classes — a setting where gossip, theories, and social allegiances around the Paradise death are exchanged (005-015)(005-030)."

memes.label: Wrychester Golf Club
memes.description: The golf course and clubhouse on Wrychester Common where Bryce waylaid Mary Bewery to sow suspicion about Dr. Ransford, and where Glassdale was later sighted during the investigation. (010-041)

memes.label: "Wrychester Police"
memes.description: "The official law-enforcement body of Wrychester, led by Detective-Sergeant Mitchington, which conducts the investigation into the suspicious deaths in Paradise and the Cathedral Close within the limits of official procedure and available evidence (003-054)(004-024)."

memes.label: "Wrychester Police Station"
memes.description: "The headquarters of the Wrychester police force, where Inspector Mitchington and the Superintendent coordinate the investigations into the deaths of John Braden and Collishaw, and where evidence and suspects are brought for examination (003-054)(004-024)."
